column2:
This section will help you understand how damage gets calculated in Octopath Traveler 2. The following formulas are simplified version which abstract [Ability Ratio] and [Invocation Ratio]. You will be able to find theses values in the Job section if you scroll to the right side, should you want to push your understanding even further.

    Damage_Output = [Base Damage] x [Skill Ratio] x [Level Multiplier] x [Multiplier_XYZ] x [Random(0.98 to 1.02)]

Basically the [Base Damage] gets multiplied by various factors. Let's first see how base damage gets calculated.
Depending which skill you are using it will either work with Physical or Elemental Attack/Defense respectively

    [Base Damage] (Physical) = [Phys. Att.] - ([Phys. Def]  * [Def. Mod] / 2)
    [Base Damage] (Elemental) = [Elem. Att.] - ([Elem. Def] * [Def. Mod] / 2)

The [Def. Mod] (defense modifier), is speccific to each skills (see the Job section to get values for each skills). This is a modifier that penetrate defense (negative value) or in other cases it actually enhance your target's defense (positive value).

You can increase your base Damage by leveling up, eating nuts or equipping better gear.
Understanding the formulas, it takes 2 points in defense to negate 1 point in base offense (not factoring Def. Mod). The weakest enemies generally have around 75 to 90 in their defense stats. Thoughest enemies, some of them have around 400 to 500 in their defense stat and sometimes even higher. For speccific info check out the "Enemies" tab.

To get [Skill Ratio] information you can check the "Job" section where every skills are displayed.
The [Level Multiplier] information is displayed further down below in this section.
The [Random(0.98 to 1.02)], this just means the output will have a randomize element in it making the output varies around 4% (2% below or 2% above)
The [Multiplier_XYZ], is all the other multiplier that can increase damage. They all stack up multiplicatively unless they are of the same kind. For example if you have 2 weapon that adds +30% damage, combined together they actually add +60%. All these multiplier will be described in their respective section.

Other Multiplier
    [Crit]: if you successfully land a critical strike, damage is 'usually' increased by +25%. However there's a few exception... Warrior skill "Enervating Slash" has its crit multiplier only at +20%. Also many "Learned skills" from hikari has Crit. Multiplier at +50% (see the Learned Skill section to get details). Normally Spells doesn't crit unless you get a buff/debuff that force a crit hit. (Spells also crit at +25% in that case)
    [Weakness]: if you hit a weakness, damage is increased by +30%
    [Broken]: if target is broken, damage is increased by +100% (Weakness multiplier doesn't stack over this)
    [phys/elem. Attack buff]: attack buff increase damage by +50%
    [phys/elem. Defense debuff]: defense debuff increase damage by +50%
    [phys/elem. Attack debuff]: attack debuff decrease damage by -33%
    [phys/elem. Defense buff]: defense buff decrease damage by -33%

Because people don't take the time to test and verify the numbers, there is misinformation that has spread everywhere on the net regarding grinding in general in OT2. Here I will explain how the game does the math so you don't waste your time doing unnecessary steps.

Exp / JP:
  - Extra Experience (support): +10% Exp
  - Hard Worker (support): +10% JP
  - EXP Augmentor (accessory): +50% Exp
  - JP Augmentor (accessory): +50% JP
  - Life in the Shadows (support): +50% Exp / JP (night only)
  - Brooch of Joy (accessory): +50% Exp / JP
The support skills and accessories all add up togheter. If you equip everything you get +160% Exp / JP.

  - Treasure Smell (Alrond skill): X2, X10, X100 Exp/JP
  - Bewildering Grace (Dancer skill): X2, X5, X100 Exp/JP
  - Jewel Cake (item): X1.2 Exp only
Multipliers from any of the above (Treasure Smell, Bewildering Grace and Jewel Cake), you only get to keep the highest. For example, let's say you hit X5 with Bewildering Grace, then you summon Alrond and get X100 and then you use the Jewel Cake item, in the end you'll only get X100. Anyone saying you can get X200 is spreading lies, and whoever tells you to use Jewel Cake after getting your X100 Exp multiplier never tested it nor did the math.

All the bonuses above add up additively. This means if you get all the above equiped and get X100 multiplier either Exp or JP. In total, this will net you a multiplier of X101.6 (+100.6%). Once you get access to Alrond's Treasure Smell skill, all the other Exp / JP bonus becomes insignificant, and I would suggest you find yourself something else to equip.
Again in OT2, Bewildering Grace's odds of obtaining X100 multiplier is very low (0.1%) and not worth using in normal scenario. However Alrond's chances of getting X100 is pretty high, not sure the odds exactly but probably 1 in 6 or somewhere near 10-15%.

Best way to farm for Exp in the end game is to summon Alrond against Caits, Octopuffs and Mini-Bosses (or the one-time encounter against Foreign Assassin who'll teach you your Hired Help option at 50K). Make sure you equip yourself with Cait Powder, Octopuff Pots, and More Rare Monsters (support) to maximize the time it takes to get a good encounter, other than that equip stuff that will help you kill the enemy faster so you don't waste time.


Money:
  - Grows on Trees (support): +10%
  - Prosperity Charm (accessory): +10%
All the money you make using Collect, Bifelgan's Bounty, or bonus money from Bewildering Grace doesn't get multiplied by any of the above perk.
While you can only have one Grows on Trees support skill effective at any time, the accessories can be stacked (additively). If you equip both Prosperity Charm, you get +20%, then add Grows on Trees support skill and you're now at +30% at the end of combat.
